drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is praised across nearly every review as rich and smooth, with specific positive mentions of the iced caramel coffee and dirty chai.
drink.quote: rich, smooth, and always perfectly made

food.name: Omelet with toast
food.reason: Only one specific food item received clear positive feedback; the Carmela pancake was criticized as not worth the price, while the omelet was genuinely enjoyed.
food.quote: tried the omelet with toast and really enjoyed it

reviewSample.text:
I was looking for a quiet spot to get some work done on a Saturday morning, and this cafe came highly recommended. It's a busy little place, offering a solid cafe menu, coffee, and reliable Wi-Fi. The owners are hands-on, helping out behind the counter and supporting staff with clean-up, which makes the space feel welcoming and well-run.

Being in a shopping center, parking is available but can be limited depending on the time of day. The inside is small, but the layout works well for small groups. They have conference-style tables, high tops, and charging ports. During the festive season, the cozy decor adds a cheerful touch.

The staff were approachable and helpful, answering our questions clearly. They serve all-day brunch, sandwiches, flatbreads, salads, and bowls, so there's something for everyone.

My friend tried the omelet with toast and really enjoyed it. I went for the Carmela pancake. Honestly, I expected a bit more for $14. It was a small cornbread skillet topped with strawberries, blueberries, granola, and maple syrup. It looked great, but I felt it wasn't worth the price; a sandwich might have been a better choice, but the staff had recommended this.

We also had a small misunderstanding where I was charged for both dishes as we discussed options, but the staff were prompt in correcting it and issuing a refund.

Drinks included a Matcha and a Tiramisu, with dairy-free options available. The coffee was rich and smooth. I'm not a huge coffee drinker, but I genuinely enjoyed it.

Overall, the cafe has a relaxed, cozy vibe. It's the kind of place where you can work, catch up with friends, there was even a small group having Bible study on the patio.

Where food meets adventure see you back at the table!
IG: @ShallonzTable

Carmela Coffee is one of our favorite spots in Parkland. My son has breakfast there at least once a week, and it’s become a special part of our routine.
I absolutely love their coffee — rich, smooth, and always perfectly made. I often come with my friends to enjoy a relaxing morning together.
The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, and the staff is always friendly and attentive.
It’s the perfect place to start the day, whether for a quick coffee or a long chat with good company. ☕

Carmela Coffee is my go-to spot for coffee and breakfast, though they offer an impressive selection of food all day. The customer service is outstanding—Santiago, in particular, is fantastic. He recognizes repeat customers and often has my to-go order ready immediately. The environment is warm and inviting, making it a great place to relax. I highly recommend Carmela Coffee.

drink.reason: Reviews mention drinks are excellent but no specific drink is named.

food.name: Smashburger
food.reason: The Smashburger is specifically named in two separate reviews with positive praise—described as juicy, classic, and cooked just right.
food.quote: the smash burger was cooked just right—juicy, classic, and delicious

reviewSample.text:
Dinner at Dear Olivia Bar & Kitchen was an enjoyable experience from start to finish. The menu offered an excellent variety, and every dish we tried was prepared beautifully. The coconut shrimp was crisp and flavorful, the Asian chicken wings were perfectly seasoned, and the guacamole and chips were fresh and satisfying. The crab cake stood out as especially well done, and the smash burger was cooked just right—juicy, classic, and delicious. The Branzino was fresh and enjoyable.

The atmosphere is beautiful yet casual, creating a welcoming, family-friendly environment that works just as well for a laid-back dinner as it does for watching sports or spending time with friends.

Service was exceptional, thanks in large part to Maddi, who was attentive, friendly, and professional throughout the evening. Her excellent service truly elevated the experience. Overall, Dear Olivia Bar & Kitchen is a really nice place to eat, drink, and unwind.

First time trying Dear Olivia’s and it did not disappoint. Everything was so yummy and tasty. We started off with the shrimp tacos. I got the Smashburger and my husband got the shrimp and grits. Everything was very good . We sat on the patio  and it was on the lake with a nice water fountain . Our serve John  was great from finish to start . Being in the restaurant industry it’s always nice getting great service. Thank you and we will see you soon.

We visited Dear Olivia for the first time yesterday for lunch. We were greeted as soon as we walked in and sat in the outside tables by the water as per our request. We were quickly greeted by our server Dana who had a big beautiful welcoming smile as she approached our table. Dana was friendly and knowledgeable about the menu and even recommended a couple of drinks for us. She kept checking in on us throughout our lunch and made small talk with my daughter who is pregnant. Thank you Dana, it’s people like you that makes meals enjoyable and keeps customers coming back to an establishment again!

food.name: Avocado toast

reviewSample.text: Food and ambiance are good. I’ve come 5-6 times now. Drinks are never good. I’ve tried the iced coffees, their signature, latte, decaf and regular. But they are always bland, watery, just not good. Today I tried the new nitro hibiscus and my sister the iced matcha thinking maybe a non-coffee would do better, but both were rough. I think they’ll make it with the great location and good food, and I’ll keep trying.
Beautiful interior with great service. Lots of seating. Food and drinks were good!
Great food and coffee empanadas were tasty and fresh the chicken sandwich was great will be back soon
